feed.title: SAP Community - BW SAP HANA Data Warehousing
feed.subtitle: BW SAP HANA Data Warehousing Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Loading BW Data into ACDOCU (SAP HANA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm looking for the most efficient way to load data from our BW  into the ACDOCU table in SAP HANA. Could anyone shed some light on the following:

Standard vs. Custom: Is there a standard SAP-provided mechanism for this kind of data transfer, or do we need to involve our ABAP team for a custom solution?

Any insights, tips, or code snippets would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: CDS Entity View as data source in SAP Data Services Designer
entry.content:
I am experiencing an issue with using a CDS entity view as a data source in SAP Data Services Designer. When attempting to import it from SAP S/4HANA to BODS, the CDS entity view name is not visible, unlike classic CDS views.

Note: The CDS entity view has OData.publish active, and the service is also active.

Could someone explain why the CDS entity view is not appearing and how I might resolve this issue?

entry.title: RTE:[89134] Proxy server connect: Host unreachable
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am encountering  the following error when trying Connecting to SAP Datasphere from python via hdbcli

(5921, 'Unable to connect remote source: [SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB SO][HDBODBC] Communication link failure;-10709 Connection failed (RTE:[89134] Proxy server connect: Host unreachable)

this is what I am trying to do -
cursor = connection.cursor()
dbExampleView = "VW_ZZ_PCARD_HOLDERS"
sql_command = f'select top 10000 * from "{dbConsumptionSchema}"."{dbExampleView}"'
print(sql_command)
cursor.execute(sql_command)
rows = cursor.fetchall()
df = pd.Dataframe(rows)
display(df) 

How can I fix this error? and what could be causing this problem

entry.title: SAP BW 7.5 to SAP Datasphere: Why It’s Time to Skip SAP BW/4HANA and Embedded Analytics
entry.content:
Why Migrate from SAP BW 7.5 to SAP Datasphere?

1. End of Support:
SAP BW 7.5: Mainstream maintenance ends in 2027, with extended support until 2030.
Long-term Viability: After 2030, SAP BW will become obsolete, with no further innovations expected.

2. Innovation:
SAP Datasphere offers advanced features like AI integration, Data Fabric, Data Marketplace, and real-time analytics—none of which are available in SAP BW 7.5.

3. Skipping SAP BW/4HANA:
Limited Innovation: SAP BW/4HANA focuses on traditional data warehousing and lacks modern capabilities like AI, data democratization, or seamless cloud integration.
Similar Transition Effort: Migrating to SAP BW/4HANA requires significant effort and cost, similar to moving to SAP Datasphere. However, SAP Datasphere positions you directly in a future-proof, cloud-based environment.
Future Roadmap: SAP is heavily investing in SAP Datasphere as its flagship data solution, whereas BW/4HANA will not see major advancements in cutting-edge technologies.

4. Skipping SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics:
Scope Limitations: Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA is designed for operational reporting within the S/4HANA environment. It lacks the breadth and depth needed for complex, enterprise-wide analytics.
Data Integration: Embedded Analytics focuses on real-time insights within S/4HANA but does not support extensive data integration across diverse sources like SAP Datasphere does.
-Scalability: For large-scale, cross-functional analytics and data management, SAP Datasphere offers a more scalable and comprehensive solution.

5. Future-Proofing:
- **Data Strategy Alignment**: With SAP Datasphere, you align with SAP’s broader strategy of integrating modern data concepts like Data Mesh and knowledge graphs, preparing your business for the future.

 Pros and Cons of Migrating to SAP Datasphere

Pros:
 Modern Capabilities: Access to the latest data management technologies.
Scalability: Built for the cloud, allowing seamless scaling.
Integration: Easier integration with other SAP cloud solutions like SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).
Flexibility: Supports diverse data use cases beyond traditional reporting.

Cons:
Migration Complexity: Moving from on-premise to cloud can be challenging.
Initial Cost: Investment in new infrastructure and training.
Learning Curve: Teams need to adapt to new tools and processes.

Steps for Migrating to SAP Datasphere

1. Assessment:
Inventory: Identify the data models, reports, and processes currently running in SAP BW 7.5.
Business Needs: Align migration with business goals and future data strategies.

2.Preparation
Data Cleansing: Clean up and optimize existing data to ensure smooth migration.
Training: Upskill your team on SAP Datasphere tools and features.

3.Proof of Concept (PoC)
Pilot Project: Start with a small, non-critical workload to test the migration process.

4. Data Migration:
Replication Flow: Use replication flow for real-time data transfer from SAP BW to SAP Datasphere.
Transformation: Map and transform data structures from SAP BW to match the new SAP Data sphere model.

5.Validation
Data Consistency: Ensure data integrity post-migration.
Performance Testing: Validate the performance of reports and analytics.

6.Go Live:
Cutover: Move production workloads to SAP Datasphere.
Monitor: Continuously monitor performance and resolve any post-migration issues.

7.Optimisation
Leverage New Features:Implement advanced capabilities such as AI/ML and Data Marketplace in your workflows.
Continuous Improvement: Regularly refine data models and processes.

When to Migrate?

Start Early: Ideally, begin planning by 2025 to allow sufficient time for a smooth transition before SAP BW 7.5 support ends.
Staggered Approach: Gradually move workloads, allowing time to adapt and optimize.

Conclusion

By skipping SAP BW/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics, and moving directly to SAP Datasphere, you ensure your data strategy is built on a modern, scalable, and future-proof foundation that aligns with the latest advancements in data management and analytics.

entry.title: SAP CDS view move from NONDESNOTCONNECTED to a new Catalog
entry.content:
Good morning,

I created a SAP CDS in S/4.  The CDS view is in BW under NONDESNOTCONNECTED. I want to move the CDS to its proper catalog. We're on BW on HANA.

Right mouse on the object does not provide an option to move the object.

Can you please describe the process for moving the CDS view into the correct catalog?

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: How to Understand the HANA Generated CalculationScenario
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have below design 

HANA CVs(Ex: CV1,CV2 ) With Input Parameters >> Used as Sources In HANA BW HCPR (EX:CP1) >> Generate External CV for HCPR CP1

The Generated CV ( "_SYS_BIC"."system-local.bw.bw2hana/CP1" with Input Params ) Internally use some generated object as Source which will be in this pattern :  "<Schema-Name>"."0BW:BIA:CP1"  

When I open that object , It has some script which seems to be called as Calculation Scenario.

Questions:
a) How can we under stand that Calculation Scenario Script , As its not SQL.

b) When I use this Generated CV ("system-local.bw.bw2hana/CP1") of CP1  as Source in any further CV , Will that Input Params gets pushed back to the Base CV's ( CV1,CV2 base nodes ) Via CalculationScenario Script ? 

 

Br

Kiran K

entry.title: how to check 0equipment attr and Text data in hana view enabled as it is time dependent
How to get the details of the Tools accessing HANA Models
entry.content:
Hello All,

I would like to know the best way to know the details of the external tools like Power BI , Tableau etc which are accessing the HANA models/BW Models actively.

I assume if any BW Models is being accessed externally , their details will be captured in the statistic tables/cubes with that we can confirm if any tools are actively calling any BW objects based on application User names.

How can we confirm the same for HANA Models/Tables in the HANA DB? Is there any way to get those usage statistics ?

 

Br

Kiran K

entry.content:

Can you please share Fiori Apps for Data Volume Management?

entry.title: How to add a calculated field to HCPR?
entry.content:
Hello to all of you,

How to create a calculated field in my Composite Provider, when the additional functions in the context menu aren't available? Should I activate any feature in Eclipse?

 Kind Regards,
HH

entry.title: Join BSEG, BKPF, EKBE and EKPO
entry.content:
Hello, I am working on a sql S/4 HANA db.

Anyone has an idea how to join the tables BSEG, BKPF with EKBE and EKPO. 

Thank you.

entry.title: How to access comments in BW/4HANA for reporting purposes
entry.content:
I have used the commenting function in #BW/4HANA for data point comments in a query. Technically, the document store object is used. The following image shows my architecture

I have recorded two comments. Now I would like to evaluate the comments in a separate report or process them with machine learning later.

 

The question now is how I can access the comments in the #BW/4HANA backend. When I look at the data (tables via SE11) in DSO /1BW/DOAHD5Z, I see all the data around the comment, but not the comment itself.

 

The question now is, in which table or where are the comments themselves stored and how can I access them to build a report based on them. The report shall give me only all comments.

entry.title: Data source Enhancement for the 2lis_02_SCL with BADI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Kindly please help me to enhance the data source 2lis_02_SCL Data source from the table EKES field EINDT where Confirmation Category (EBTYP) = 'AB'. 

let me know how to write the BADI code.

 

Thanks and Regards,

Venkat.

entry.title: Trouble while downloading SAP HANA studio setup from SAP software downloads.
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

As per note 2375176 by using the SAP HANA version we need to find the corresponding / required SAP HANA studio version. 

 

Now, as per the HANA version only I have to download the SAP HANA Studio setup from SAP software downloads. But, suppose my version is 2.00.059.xx ( xx= 0.00 to 0.nn) . Based on different xx's HANA studio is different. Now, when I checked the content info of one setup file IMC_STUDIO2_259_14-80000323.SAR , it comprises of all the 2.00.059.xx HANA revisions . So, ultimately which SAP HANA studio version will this setup correspond to 2.3.70 or 2.3.74 or something else. This is the case with other HANA versions also.

example : For 2.00.059.14 HANA version HANA studio is 2.3.78 . For 2.00.059.09 HANA version HANA studio is 2.3.74.

 

Regards,

 

Beena

entry.title: Datasource 0CRM_SALES_ACT_1 & 0PM_FLEET is deprecated and doesn't have alternate CDS views
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are currently migrating ECC to S4HANA and there are two datasources which doesn't have any alternate CDS views recommended by SAP( 0CRM_SALES_ACT_1 & 0PM_FLEET ). They are deprecated, has anyone have any solution for this or came across same scenario? what was done in this case? any help will be really appreciated

entry.title: Do not update delta pointer if not changed since the last update.
entry.content:
Hi Experts.

I have created a data source in ECC which delta is based on a time stamp field.

The data source feeds from a custom table in the same system which is updated every five minutes with IDOC information (EDIDC, EDIDS, EDID4) via custom program (job).

During pick hours this job may take up to 3 hours per execution. But usually 5 min should be enough.

In BW the delta upload is scheduled also every 5 min. However during pick hours the delta keeps running every 5 min and since it does not find any changes in the database it brings nothing from the source. However the delta pointer is updated in source system, and it will keep being updating for 3 hours more bringing no data.

Once the 3 hours job execution is finished in source system, the next delta execution works fine but with the wrong delta pointer. The problem is that during those 3 hours the delta pointer has been updated in the source system and it is skipping all records updated during those 3 hours creating a gap in the upload to BW.

Before changing the delta pointer to a "numeric" pointer, may I ask: Is there any way via user exit to control the delta pointer update in source system as saying: "if there is no change in the delta since the last update do not update data pointer". That way once the 3 hours pick are over, the next delta will pick up in the latest valid pointer without skipping records.

Thanks for your comments.

entry.title: What should be the correct value for "global_allocation_limit" ?
entry.content:
1. Currently host has  “global_allocation_limit” is set to 0 .

2. For the host

    DB Allocated Memory is 361.98 GB (as per screenshot below) vs

    Licensed Memory is 245 GB 

    Total Physical Memory on the host is 377.89 GB

3 Current Memory allocation for SAP Hana Production System is below as per below screenshots.

BECAUSE, Current DB Allocated Memory  (361.98 GB) which is lot higher than Licensed Memory is (245 GB) seeing random errors like "Cache Refresh Failure" & "No DB to sign".

What should be the correct value for "global_allocation_limit" ?

This is our production and critical system so your input & feedback is greatly appreciated. 

Happy to share more details in case if you need.

 

Thanks in advance for your time and feedback !

JS.

entry.title: 0MATERIAL Structure Modification
entry.content:
Hi All,

For some context we are working on SAP BW 7.5 on HANA, on a "classic" DEV->REC->PREPRODUCTION->PRODUCTION environment

For the need of a project we did some modification of the infoObject 0MATERIAL (added a few attributes), These modifications were transported to our PreProduction environnement but will not be transported to the production until some time.

What can we expect from this modification of the 0MATERIAL Structure, is it still possible to transport change of Transformation or ADSO (containing 0MATERIAL) from our dev environment to the production environment?

We are worried that the modification of 0MATERIAL prevents us to do development on our system until the transport to production

for information, in the production system we have access to the "RSDDB_LOGINDEX_CREATE" program

Thanks for your response

Regards.

entry.title: DTP Load Failure due to Overflow converting Issue
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to load DTP on BW4HANA platform ,but DTP is failing due to overflow converting issue.

The same DTP works fine when i selective load the data but when i full load ,its failing .

below is the error message.

Not able to exactly find out erroneous records ,so its becoming very tough to extract the data.

Expecting quick response.

Thanks in advance,

Rajeev

entry.title: SAP BW DTP dynamic filter multiple fields
entry.content:
Hello

is there a way to set the SAP BW DTP filter for multiple fields at once, or do i have to create for each field a routine?
